retry_reason: This is a put wrapper: after the call the object is considered dead. Retrying would cause a use-after-free as the reference count is decremented exactly once and the object is freed.
contract_summary: This function performs the final release of a bpf_prog object. It cleans up auxiliary data structures (kallsyms, BTF, module references, line info, etc.) and then calls __bpf_prog_put_rcu to decrement the reference count and free the program. The caller must not access the prog pointer after this call. Based on the 'deferred' parameter, the actual freeing may be deferred via RCU callbacks (for sleepable programs, rcu_tasks_trace; otherwise vanilla RCU). The function does not return a value; the caller must ensure that no other reference to the prog exists after this (or the final reference is being released).

judge.reason: The function's entire body consists of cleanup actions (kallsyms, btf_put, module_put, kvfree/kfree on auxiliary fields) followed by a call to __bpf_prog_put_rcu, which decrements the reference count and frees the prog. There is no unrelated business logic; all operations are part of the final object release.

check.reason: All three extracted put operations are consistent with the function code. The access paths correctly trace from parameter 1 (prog) through aux to the respective objects (btf, mod, attach_btf) and their refcount fields. The member type chains match the actual struct types, and segment counts are equal. The directions (put) match the callee semantics (btf_put, module_put, and btf_put again for attach_btf).
